provenance.task: C02 P1.5 — body-keyword augmentation for the answer-key set-cover predictor
provenance.purpose: Maps a canonical cooking_skills / main_ingredients value to a lowercase, case-insensitive regex over the lesson BODY (content_text). A lesson is a keyword-CANDIDATE for value V iff a pattern exists for V and new RegExp(pattern, 'i').test(body). The set-cover predictor (sample-answer-key.ts) takes candidates(V) = floor-over-current-tags(V) ∪ body-keyword-scan(V) so that values with near-zero current carriers (added specifics; the empty-floor tail Grilling/Melons; the low-floor tail Fermenting/Wrapping & rolling/Stone fruits/Peanut butter/Fennel) can still reach the ≥2× coverage guarantee.
provenance.grounding: Session 4 read-only PROD census proved ≥2× is universally achievable for all 93 canonical values under floor ∪ keyword. The 7 low-floor values below each had 5-28 body-keyword candidates. These patterns are the grounding-derived seeds.
provenance.extensibility: P2 (the real pilot run) MAY extend this file if the real corpus reveals a coverage gap for a value with no pattern here — adding a pattern is a data-only change. Patterns are matched case-insensitively against the raw body text; keep them lowercase by convention but the 'i' flag makes case moot.
provenance.note: This is a SAMPLING heuristic only — it never writes a tag. It just widens the candidate pool the deterministic set-cover draws from so every value is representable. Over-broad patterns only cost a slightly larger candidate pool; they never produce a wrong tag.
